countries.name: Australia
countries.topIncomeNote: Includes the 2% Medicare levy: 45% top marginal rate on taxable income over AUD 190,000 plus the 2% levy that applies to most residents (PwC). No state income taxes.
countries.corporateNote: Standard rate (Tax Foundation 2025); 25% applies to 'small-medium business' entities (PwC).
countries.vatNote: Goods and services tax (GST) per PwC VAT quick chart.
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2023 figure; provisional 2024 data for Australia was not available in OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries — Australia, taxes on personal income

countries.name: Austria
countries.topIncomeNote: The 55% top rate applies only to income above EUR 1,000,000; the next bracket (EUR 104,859-1,000,000) is taxed at 50% (PwC 2026).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; third-highest in the OECD.

countries.name: Belgium
countries.topIncomeNote: 50% federal top rate plus an average communal surcharge of 7% of the income tax due (communal rates range 0-9%), per PwC (income year 2025); matches Tax Foundation's 2026 combined figure.
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

countries.name: Brazil
countries.topIncomeNote: Top rate of the federal personal income tax schedule (PwC headline PIT rate).
countries.corporateNote: Combined 34%: corporate income tax (IRPJ) at 25% (15% plus 10% surtax) plus social contribution on net profits (CSLL) at 9% (PwC).
countries.vatNote: No single national VAT. Per PwC: state VAT (ICMS) normally 17-20%, federal PIS/COFINS combined 3.65% (cumulative) or 9.25% (non-cumulative), federal excise (IPI) typically 5-30%, municipal service tax (ISS) 2-5%.
countries.taxToGdpNote: OECD Revenue Statistics in Latin America and the Caribbean 2026 country note; general government, includes social security contributions.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - Brazil
OECD Revenue Statistics in LAC 2026 - Brazil

countries.name: Canada
countries.topIncomeNote: Combined federal (33%) plus Ontario top rate for 2026 (PwC: 53.5%). Provincial top rates vary; the highest combined rate is 54.8% in Newfoundland and Labrador.
countries.corporateNote: Tax Foundation 2025 combined rate (25.98%): 15% federal plus provincial/territorial corporate taxes (8%-15%, not federally deductible).
countries.vatNote: Federal GST is 5%; combined federal plus provincial sales taxes (HST/PST/QST) range from 5% to 15% (PwC VAT quick chart).
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries — Canada, taxes on personal income

countries.name: Chile
countries.topIncomeNote: Top rate of the progressive second-category/global complementary tax (PwC).
countries.corporateNote: 27% under the general (partially integrated) regime (Tax Foundation 2025); 25% applies under the SME regime (PwC).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC VAT quick chart.
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

countries.topIncomeNote: Top rate of the progressive individual income tax on comprehensive income (PwC headline PIT rate).
countries.corporateNote: Standard enterprise income tax rate (PwC headline CIT rate).
countries.vatNote: Standard VAT rate 13% for most goods; reduced rates of 9% and 6% apply depending on the goods or services (PwC).
countries.taxToGdpNote: OECD Revenue Statistics in Asia and the Pacific 2025 country note; general government, includes social security contributions.

countries.topIncomeNote: Top progressive rate for residents (PwC). No sub-central income tax on employment income.
countries.corporateNote: National rate; no sub-central corporate income tax (Tax Foundation 2025).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC VAT quick chart.
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; fell 2.2 percentage points from 2023, the largest OECD decline that year.

countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

countries.corporateNote: Distribution-based system: 22% applies only when profits are distributed; retained earnings are untaxed (PwC).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).

countries.name: Finland
countries.topIncomeNote: National top rate of 37.5% on income above EUR 52,100 plus average municipal tax of 7.5% (range 4.7-10.9%) for 2026, per PwC. Excludes church tax (1-2.25%) and the capped public-broadcasting tax. Down sharply from 51.8% in 2025 (Tax Foundation).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

countries.name: France
countries.topIncomeNote: Combined figure per Tax Foundation's 2026 table (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/). The statutory top bracket is 45%, plus the exceptional contribution on high incomes (3-4% above EUR 250,000/500,000) and CSG/CRDS-type charges; from 2025 a differential contribution (CDHR) enforces a 20% minimum tax on high incomes (PwC).
countries.corporateNote: Includes surcharges. A temporary exceptional contribution raises the rate to 36.1% for the first fiscal year ending on or after 31 December 2025 (Tax Foundation).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; second-highest in the OECD.

countries.name: Germany
countries.topIncomeNote: 45% top rate (taxable income above EUR 277,826 single) plus the 5.5% solidarity surcharge levied on the tax = 47.5% (PwC; Tax Foundation 2026). Church tax (8-9% of the income tax, for church members) excluded.
countries.corporateNote: 15% federal CIT plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge (15.825%) plus municipal trade tax varying roughly 8.75-20.3%; overall burden is ~30% in Berlin and ~33% in Munich (PwC). Federal CIT is legislated to fall stepwise from 2028. 30.1% is Tax Foundation's 2026 combined figure.
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

countries.name: Greece
countries.topIncomeNote: Top marginal rate of 44%; no sub-central income tax (Tax Foundation combined figure for 2026 is also 44.0%).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 38.9%).

countries.name: Hong Kong
countries.topIncomeNote: Salaries tax: progressive rates run 2-17%, but total tax is capped at the two-tiered standard rates - 15% on the first HKD 5 million of net income and 16% on the remainder (2025/26). Taxpayers pay the lower of the two computations, so 16% is the effective top statutory rate (PwC).
countries.corporateNote: Profits tax: 16.5% for corporations, 15% for unincorporated businesses (PwC headline rates).
countries.vatNote: No VAT/GST (PwC lists the standard VAT rate as 'NA').
countries.taxToGdpNote: OECD Revenue Statistics in Asia and the Pacific 2025 country note for Hong Kong (China); general government.

countries.name: Hungary
countries.topIncomeNote: Flat 15% personal income tax; no sub-central income tax (Tax Foundation combined figure for 2026 is also 15.0%).
countries.corporateNote: Lowest headline corporate rate in the OECD (Tax Foundation 2025 combined figure: 9.0%).
countries.vatNote: Highest standard VAT rate in the OECD; per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 35.0%).

countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

countries.name: India
countries.topIncomeNote: PwC headline maximum marginal rate under the default new personal tax regime: 30% top slab plus 25% surcharge plus 4% health and education cess = 39%.
countries.corporateNote: Domestic companies: base rate 25% or 30% depending on turnover, plus applicable surcharge and cess; concessional 22% or 15% regimes available subject to conditions (PwC).
countries.vatNote: GST standard rate 18%; GST has multiple slabs ranging from 5% to 28% depending on the goods or services supplied (PwC).
countries.taxToGdpNote: World Bank series 'Tax revenue (% of GDP)' (GC.TAX.TOTL.GD.ZS), central government only - excludes state taxes, so the general-government ratio is substantially higher. 2022 is the most recent non-empty value.

countries.name: Ireland
countries.topIncomeNote: Income tax top rate is 40% (on income above EUR 44,000 single, PwC 2026); the 48.0% combined figure per Tax Foundation's 2026 table (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/) additionally reflects surtaxes (the Universal Social Charge).
countries.corporateNote: Lowest headline corporate rate among these countries, per Tax Foundation's 2026 table.
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; the ratio fell from 27.7% in 2010, the largest decline in the OECD over that period.

countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).

countries.corporateNote: 24% IRES (PwC) plus the standard 3.9% regional production tax (IRAP); 27.8% is the Tax Foundation 2025 combined figure (27.81%). IRAP varies by region and industry.
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).

countries.name: Japan

countries.corporateNote: Tax Foundation 2025 combined national plus local rate (29.74%); the headline national CIT rate alone is 23.2% (PwC).
countries.vatNote: Consumption tax (Japan's VAT) per PwC VAT quick chart; a reduced 8% rate applies to certain items.
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2023 figure; provisional 2024 social security contribution data for Japan was not available in OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

countries.corporateNote: Distribution-based system: 20% payable only when profits are distributed (PwC).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).

countries.corporateNote: Standard rate 17% from 1 January 2026 (PwC); Tax Foundation lists 16% for 2025. Credit institutions pay an additional 5%.
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).

countries.corporateNote: Maximum combined rate for 2026: 19% headline IRC (mainland) plus municipal surtax (derrama municipal, up to 1.5%) and state surtax (derrama estadual, up to 9% on profits above EUR 35 million), per PwC. Tax Foundation's 2025 combined figure was 30.5% (20% headline that year).

countries.name: Russia
countries.topIncomeNote: Five-band progressive scale (13/15/18/20/22%) introduced 1 January 2025; the 22% top rate applies to annual income above RUB 50 million. PwC no longer publishes a Russia summary, so figures are from legal advisories covering the 2024 tax reform law.
countries.corporateNote: General corporate income tax rate raised from 20% to 25% from 1 January 2025.
countries.vatNote: Standard VAT rate increased from 20% to 22% under Federal Law No. 425-FZ (adopted 28 November 2025), applying from 2026; a 10% preferential rate is retained for some products.
countries.taxToGdpNote: World Bank series 'Tax revenue (% of GDP)' (GC.TAX.TOTL.GD.ZS), central government only - excludes sub-national taxes and social contributions, so general-government burden is higher.

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
countries.topIncomeNote: No individual income tax scheme; employment income earned in Saudi Arabia is not taxed. Non-residents' Saudi-source income with no permanent establishment is taxed under withholding tax rules (PwC).
countries.corporateNote: 20% corporate income tax applies to the share of a resident company owned by non-Saudi/non-GCC persons and to non-residents with Saudi-source income; Saudi and GCC nationals' shareholdings pay Zakat at 2.5% of the Zakat base instead. Oil and hydrocarbon production is taxed at 50-85% (PwC).
countries.vatNote: Standard VAT rate 15% since 1 July 2020 (5% before that) (PwC).
countries.taxToGdpNote: World Bank series 'Tax revenue (% of GDP)' (GC.TAX.TOTL.GD.ZS), central government only; excludes oil revenue collected through non-tax channels.

countries.vatNote: Standard VAT rate (PwC).

countries.name: South Korea
countries.topIncomeNote: Combined top rate stated by PwC: 45% national top rate plus local income tax assessed at 10% of the PIT rates (4.5% at the top bracket).
countries.corporateNote: Combined 25% national top rate plus 2.5% local income tax for fiscal years starting on or after 1 Jan 2026 (PwC). Tax Foundation's 2025 combined figure was 26.4% (24% national top rate then in force).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC VAT quick chart.
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

countries.name: Spain
countries.topIncomeNote: Top rates vary by autonomous community: the combined rate is the sum of the state scale and each region's own scale. 47% is PwC's headline combined top rate (state withholding scale, income above EUR 300,000). Tax Foundation's 2026 figure is 54.0%, reflecting the highest regional combination.
countries.corporateNote: 25% standard rate (PwC CIT quick chart; Tax Foundation 2025 combined figure also 25.0%).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 36.4%).

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries — Spain, personal income tax
Tax Foundation — Top Personal Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026
OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 — Tax revenue trends 1965–2024

countries.name: Sweden
countries.topIncomeNote: 20% national tax on taxable income above SEK 643,000 plus an average municipal rate of about 32% (PwC 2026). Combined 52.3% per Tax Foundation's 2026 table (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/), which uses the precise average municipal rate.
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

sources.label: PwC Tax Summaries (Sweden)
Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)
OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (highlights)

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.topIncomeNote: Combined figure for a typical canton per Tax Foundation's 2026 table (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/). The federal top rate is 11.5%; cantonal and communal taxes vary widely by canton and commune (PwC), so the actual top rate depends on residence.
countries.corporateNote: Federal CIT is 8.5% on profit after tax (~7.8% pre-tax); combined federal + cantonal + communal maximum rates range from about 11.7% to 20.5% depending on canton (PwC). 19.6% is Tax Foundation's 2026 representative combined figure.
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

sources.label: PwC Tax Summaries (Switzerland)
Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)
OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (highlights)

countries.name: Turkey
countries.topIncomeNote: 40% top income tax bracket (above TRY 5,300,000 in 2026) plus stamp duty of 0.759% on gross salaries (PwC); 40.8% is the Tax Foundation 2026 combined-with-surtaxes figure.
countries.corporateNote: 25% standard rate; 30% for financial sector companies (PwC CIT quick chart).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 23.2%). OECD notes 2024 nominal revenues rose 69% amid very high inflation.

Tax Foundation — Top Personal Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026

countries.name: United Arab Emirates
countries.topIncomeNote: No personal income tax; PwC lists the headline PIT rate as 'NA' (the territory does not have the tax).
countries.corporateNote: New federal corporate income tax with a 9% headline rate (PwC headline CIT rate).
countries.vatNote: Standard VAT rate (PwC).
countries.taxToGdpNote: World Bank series 'Tax revenue (% of GDP)' (GC.TAX.TOTL.GD.ZS), central government only; most UAE government revenue comes from sources outside this series.

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.topIncomeNote: Additional rate of 45% on income over GBP 125,140 for 2026/27 in England, Wales and Northern Ireland; Scotland's top rate on employment income is 48% (PwC).
countries.vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.

sources.label: PwC Tax Summaries (United Kingdom)

countries.name: United States
countries.topIncomeNote: Federal top rate only (37% for 2025, PwC). No single combined figure: most states levy their own income tax on top (eight states have none; few state rates exceed 10%), so the combined top rate varies by state.
countries.corporateNote: Tax Foundation 2025 combined rate (25.57%): 21% federal plus average state corporate tax; state CIT ranges from 0% to 12% and is deductible against federal tax.
countries.vatNote: No national VAT; state and local sales taxes average 7.5% combined (population-weighted, 7.53% as of 1 Jan 2026, Tax Foundation: taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/sales-tax-rates/).
countries.taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025.
